name: Pennsylvania German (Pennsylvania Dutch)
notes: Grapheme inventory NOT YET SPECIFIED (stub). Pennsylvania German has NO standardised orthography: two incompatible writing systems are in current use — the Buffington-Barba system, which spells by Standard German conventions, and an English-based system (Beam / 'Deitsch' spelling) which spells by American English conventions — and the same word is spelled differently in each with no difference in pronunciation. A single letter-to-phoneme map is therefore not well-defined for the code as such; a Tier-A spec must first pick ONE orthography and declare it (the German-conventional Buffington-Barba system is the tractable choice), which has not been done and verified here. Phonologically the variety continues Palatine (Rhine Franconian) German, with the well-known shifts German /œ yː ɔʏ/ → /ɛ iː aɪ/, lenition of /p t k/ → [b d ɡ], intervocalic /b/ → [v] and post-vocalic /ɡ/ → [j].

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
